data.title: Structural Subordination Risk Looms Over iRhythm Holding Company’s Ability to Service 2029 Notes
data.description:
Irhythm Technologies (IRTC) has disclosed a new risk, in the Corporate Activity and Growth category.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The holding company’s ability to service its obligations, including guarantees of iRhythm Technologies’ 2029 Notes, is entirely dependent on cash flows upstreamed from iRhythm Technologies and its subsidiaries. Because those subsidiaries are separate legal entities with no obligation to provide dividends, loans or other payments, and such transfers may be restricted by law or contract, creditors of the holding company face meaningful structural subordination risk.

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on IRTC stock based on 12 Buys and 1 Hold.

To learn more about Irhythm Technologies’ risk factors, click here.

Merit Medical’s amended executive employment and equity award agreements could increase fixed and variable compensation obligations, particularly upon termination or change in control. This may elevate corporate governance scrutiny and dilute existing shareholders, especially if performance targets are not matched by corresponding improvements in financial results.
